reasoning: The ICE BofA Single-A US Corporate Index OAS experienced a small spike leading into February 13, 2026, reaching 0.65, likely driven in part by a dip in the S&P 500 around the same time. This 0.65 reading represents a local peak compared to late January/early February values (which hovered between 0.59 and 0.63). Since single-A corporate spreads typically exhibit short-term mean reversion, especially during periods where general conditions are tight and mostly stable (as noted by various 2026 investment outlook reports), the short-term probability (February 22) of the spread remaining strictly above the localized spike of 0.65 is lower than 50% (estimated at 36%). For the longer term (March 17), the probability naturally tends closer to 0.50 as uncertainty increases, the horizon provides more opportunity for varying market volatility, and seasonal factors may introduce different directional pressures (estimated at 44%).
